title: centaurea stoebe subsp. rhenana
intro: Centaurea stoebe subsp. rhenana, also known as European knapweed or Rhenish knapweed, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow or pink flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea stoebe subsp. rhenana include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically in the form of ointments or salves.
